page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor. It features 12 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation and a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include a GPU.

Benchmark results place this server in the average performance tier across most standard workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1418.58, while the multi-core score reaches 17309.27. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1224.9 GB/sec, which drops to 87.74 GB/sec for large block uncached reads, and 47.62 GB/sec for large block writes. In database and key-value store benchmarks, the server handles 18.53 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 4095.21 in PassMark database operations. Large language model inference performance is average for small 135M models at 719.57 prompt tokens per second but drops to the weak tier for medium 7B models at 16.55 prompt tokens per second.

This server offers a high core-to-memory density with a 1:2 ratio, making it a cost-effective option for compute-heavy tasks that do not require dedicated CPU threads. The shared CPU allocation represents a performance tradeoff, as resource contention may affect sustained peak workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving with 11.99 GB/sec throughput, intermediate database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server featuring 12 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPUs, 24.0 GB of RAM, and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server delivers average performance across standard benchmarks, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 17309.27 and a PassMark CPU score of 17934.88. It handles Redis SET operations at 18.53 million operations per second and static web serving at 11.99 GB/sec. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides high compute density, though the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention. This configuration is suitable for multi-threaded processing, static web hosting, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server with 12 shared Intel Xeon cores, 24 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, delivering average multi-core and database benchmark performance.

tagline: A high-density compute server featuring twelve shared Intel Xeon cores and integrated NVMe storage for multi-threaded workloads.
